event: mucosal resection for early gastric cancer
partial resection for left lung cancer
collected knee-high wild grass from a riverbed
visited the department of internal medicine
Glasgow Coma Scale of 14: E3, V5, M6
O2 saturation (SPO2) of 94% in room air
no remarkable head-and-neck or chest abnormalities
crusted skin lesions on the medial surface of the left thigh
freely movable adenopathy of approximately 2 cm in size without tenderness
aggregated papules in the right inguinal region
neutrophil count was considerably low at 899 /μL
activated partial thromboplastin time (APTT) of 48.2 seconds
elevations in concentrations of aspartate aminotransferase (AST) at 230 U/L
chest radiography and electrocardiography were normal
simple head-and-neck computed tomography (CT) scan revealed no remarkable abnormalities
given the physical and laboratory findings, on day 1 of hospitalization, we suspected sepsis and febrile neutropenia (FN)
administered antibiotics until the neutropenia resolved
on day 2 of hospitalization, his blood test results revealed elevated ferritin levels of 15,165 ng/mL
screened closely for potential antibodies specific for cytomegalovirus and Epstein-Barr virus (EBV)
immunohistological examination revealed a high number of CD3- and CD4-positive T cells
no EBV-encoded small RNAs (EBERs)-positive cells were detected
gradually tapered along the clinical course
developed chest pain, shivering, and fever of 39.2°C
SPO2 at 94% (6-L mask for oxygen administration)
transthoracic echocardiography showed severe left ventricular dysfunction
electrocardiogram (ECG) showed a slight ST elevation in the V1-2 leads
contrast-enhanced CT of the chest and abdomen showed no remarkable abnormalities
fulfilled the diagnostic criteria of clinically suspected myocarditis
did not perform coronary angiography (CAG) or EMB at this moment
continued respiratory and circulatory management and infection control in the ICU
submitted his serum and urine samples and throat swabs to a responsible public health center
RT-PCR analysis of the submitted serum detected 1.77×106 SFTSV RNA copies/mL
submitted pathological sections of his bone marrow to the Division of Virology at the National Institute of Infectious Disease
found infected cells that were stained positively by an anti-SFTSV-nucleoprotein (N) antibody
considered steroid pulse therapy for HLH with SFTS
did not proceed with it due to concerns regarding the risks of further immune deficiency and secondary infections
withdrew from circulatory agonist and ventilator management
ECG showed improvement of ST elevation in the V1-2 leads
re-submitted his serum and confirmed negativity for viremia
performed CAG and EMB due to an insufficient improvement in his LVEF
no coronary artery stenosis was observed on CAG
pathological evaluation of the EMB specimen revealed that the myocardium was mildly hypertrophic
active mononuclear cell infiltration adjacent to the damaged myocardium was not observed
immunohistochemistry revealed increases in the number of CD3-positive T cells (10/mm2)
on a follow-up visit 1 month after discharge, transthoracic echocardiography showed a slight improvement with an EF of 52.5%
